title: Configure a Custom Provider in Terraform HCL
description: Write Terraform HCL that configures a custom provider block, uses a local dev override, and references provider-specific resources.
starterCode:
# Write a Terraform configuration that:
# 1. Declares required_providers for 'mycompany/internalapi' version ~> 1.0
#    with source = "mycompany/internalapi"
# 2. Configures the 'internalapi' provider with:
#    - endpoint = "https://api.internal.example.com"
#    - token = var.api_token (sensitive variable)
# 3. Declares a variable 'api_token' of type string, sensitive = true
# 4. Creates a resource 'internalapi_project' named 'main_project'
#    with name = "platform-team" and team = "infrastructure"

terraform {
  required_providers {
    # Add custom provider requirement
  }
}

# Variable for API token

# Provider configuration

# Resource using custom provider
hints: Custom providers use namespace/name as source (same as public registry format)
Sensitive variables: sensitive = true prevents value from appearing in output
Pass sensitive vars to provider with token = var.api_token

validationRules.errorMessage: Declare the custom provider in required_providers
Mark the api_token variable as sensitive
Add a provider configuration block for internalapi
Set the endpoint in the provider config
Create a resource using the custom provider

simulatedOutput.success:
Initializing provider plugins...
- Finding mycompany/internalapi versions matching "~> 1.0"...
- Installing mycompany/internalapi v1.2.0...

Provider configuration validated:
  internalapi: endpoint=https://api.internal.example.com, token=(sensitive)

Terraform will perform the following actions:
  + internalapi_project.main_project will be created

Plan: 1 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.
simulatedOutput.failure: Custom provider configuration error. Check required_providers, sensitive variable, provider block, and resource type.
